prompt: Give me a reusable AnimatedCounter component that ticks a number from 0 up to a target value over about 1 second whenever the target prop changes (even if it decreases). Should work for integers only (no decimals shown). Must not add any dependencies besides what I already have (motion — the React animation library). I've seen something called AnimateNumber referenced online — is that the right tool?
assertions: Uses useMotionValue to hold the animating number
Uses useTransform with a function (e.g. () => Math.round(n.get())) to round to integer
Renders the motion value as a child of a motion component (e.g. <motion.span>{rounded}</motion.span>) — NOT via toFixed / useState + setState
Uses animate() imperative function from motion/react to drive the change
useEffect starts the animation when target changes and returns cleanup (controls.stop())
Explicitly states that AnimateNumber is a Motion+ (paid) component and declines to use it
Uses TypeScript with a typed target prop
